info.title: Crucible Gateway
info.description: Clone-and-adapt framework for high-volume metered API products.

200.description: Service is alive
Prometheus exposition format
Dependency health report

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Optional deduplication key (max 255 chars). Identical-key retries within 24 h replay the stored response without re-invoking the worker or re-billing.

200.description: Successful invocation

RateLimit-Limit.description: Per-minute request cap for the customer's plan

RateLimit-Remaining.description: Requests remaining in the current sliding window

RateLimit-Reset.description: Unix timestamp when the sliding window fully resets

X-Quota-Limit.description: Monthly billable-unit cap for the customer's plan

X-Quota-Remaining.description: Billable units remaining in the current calendar month

X-Quota-Reset.description: Unix timestamp when the monthly quota resets (UTC month-end)

401.description: Unauthorized — missing or invalid API key

409.description: Idempotency conflict — concurrent request with same key

422.description: Idempotency key reused with a different request body

429.description: Rate limited or quota exceeded. RateLimit-*/X-RateLimit-* headers are always present. X-Quota-* headers are present only on quota-triggered 429s.

RateLimit-Limit.description: Per-minute request cap for the customer's plan

RateLimit-Remaining.description: Requests remaining in the current sliding window

RateLimit-Reset.description: Unix timestamp when the sliding window fully resets

X-Quota-Limit.description: Monthly billable-unit cap for the customer's plan

X-Quota-Remaining.description: Billable units remaining in the current calendar month

X-Quota-Reset.description: Unix timestamp when the monthly quota resets (UTC month-end)

get.summary: List the authenticated customer's own error-history events

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Inclusive ISO 8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD, UTC); defaults to 30 days ago

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: Inclusive ISO 8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD, UTC); defaults to today; range capped at 90 days

parameters.name: code
parameters.description: Exact error code filter, e.g. RATE_LIMITED

200.description: The caller's own error-history events, newest-first

operation.description: The /v1 route pattern that produced the error

request_payload.description: Captured request body, bounded UTF-8; null if capture was off or the row predates it

has_more.description: True when more rows exist beyond this page

400.description: Bad request — invalid date/operation/code filter or page

401.description: Unauthorized — missing or invalid API key

get.summary: List the authenticated customer's own async jobs

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter to exactly one status: queued | running | succeeded | failed | cancelled

parameters.name: operation
parameters.description: Filter to jobs enqueued with this exact operation string

200.description: The caller's own async jobs matching the given filters, newest-first

schema.description: Paginated envelope of the caller's own async jobs

billable_units.description: Present only when status is succeeded; always >= 1 (invariant #2)

error.description: Structured error {code, message}; present only when status is failed

job_id.description: Job UUID, returned by the enqueuing POST /v1/<op> call

operation.description: The opaque operation string of the POST /v1/<op> route that enqueued this job

updated_at.description: RFC3339 timestamp of the last status change

total.description: Total matching jobs across all pages

400.description: Bad request — unknown status filter, or page too large

401.description: Unauthorized — missing or invalid API key

get.summary: Get the status and result of an async job owned by the authenticated customer

200.description: Current job status; result/error/billable_units populate once the job reaches a terminal state

billable_units.description: Present only when status is succeeded; always >= 1 (invariant #2)

error.description: Structured error {code, message}; present only when status is failed

job_id.description: Job UUID, returned by the enqueuing POST /v1/<op> call

result.description: The worker's payload; present only when status is succeeded

updated_at.description: RFC3339 timestamp of the last status change

401.description: Unauthorized — missing or invalid API key

404.description: Job not found (includes ids owned by another customer)

post.summary: Cancel a still-queued async job owned by the authenticated customer
post.description: Only a job in the queued state can be cancelled this way; there is no cooperative cancellation of a job that has already started running. Cancelling transitions the job to the terminal cancelled state — it is never claimed or billed.

200.description: Job cancelled

billable_units.description: Present only when status is succeeded; always >= 1 (invariant #2)

error.description: Structured error {code, message}; present only when status is failed

job_id.description: Job UUID, returned by the enqueuing POST /v1/<op> call

result.description: The worker's payload; present only when status is succeeded

updated_at.description: RFC3339 timestamp of the last status change

401.description: Unauthorized — missing or invalid API key

404.description: Job not found (includes ids owned by another customer)

409.description: Job cannot be cancelled in its current state (not queued)

get.summary: List the authenticated customer's active API keys

200.description: The caller's active API keys (hash and full key never included)

schema.description: Paginated envelope of the caller's active API keys

items.description: API key objects for this page

expires_at.description: RFC3339 expiry set during a rotation grace window; null otherwise

last_used_at.description: RFC3339 timestamp of last successful auth; null if never used

total.description: Total active keys across all pages

401.description: Unauthorized — missing or invalid API key

delete.summary: Revoke an API key owned by the authenticated customer

401.description: Unauthorized — missing or invalid API key

404.description: Key not found (includes ids owned by another customer)

post.summary: Rotate an API key owned by the authenticated customer

grace_secs.description: Seconds the old key stays valid after rotation; clamped to [0, 604800] (7 days); default 3600 (1 hour)

200.description: Key rotated; the new full key is present only in this response

key.description: The new full API key. Returned exactly once — store it now, it cannot be retrieved again.

400.description: Bad request — malformed key id or invalid json body

401.description: Unauthorized — missing or invalid API key

404.description: Key not found (includes ids owned by another customer)

get.summary: Get the authenticated customer's current billing-period usage against their plan quota

200.description: Current-period usage — same signals the quota middleware enforces against

breakdown.description: Per-operation usage, ordered by total_units descending

cap.description: Monthly billable-unit cap for the customer's plan (0 = unlimited)

period_end.description: RFC3339 end of the current UTC billing period

period_start.description: RFC3339 start of the current UTC billing period

remaining.description: Billable units remaining this month (-1 = unlimited)

total_calls.description: Count of usage_events rows across all operations this period

total_units.description: Sum of billable_units across all operations this period

used.description: Billable units consumed so far this calendar month

401.description: Unauthorized — missing or invalid API key

get.summary: Export the authenticated customer's own per-event metered usage rows
get.description: Returns a JSON envelope by default. Direct HTTP callers (not the generated SDKs) may pass ?format=csv or an Accept: text/csv header for an RFC-4180 CSV export instead.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Inclusive ISO 8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD, UTC); defaults to 30 days ago

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: Inclusive ISO 8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD, UTC); defaults to today; range capped at 90 days

parameters.name: operation
parameters.description: Exact match against the opaque worker operation string (e.g. "echo"); max 128 characters

200.description: The caller's own usage events, newest-first, as a JSON envelope (default) or RFC-4180 CSV (format=csv or Accept: text/csv)

billable_units.description: Billable units metered for this call (always >= 1, invariant #2), encoded as a decimal string — usage.Recorder.Record accepts any int64 worker-reported value, so a JSON number could silently lose precision past Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER in JS clients

id.description: usage_events row id (BIGSERIAL, encoded as a decimal string to avoid precision loss past Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER in JS clients)

operation.description: The opaque worker operation string this usage row was recorded under (RouteDescriptor.Operation, e.g. "echo") — not a /v1/... path

has_more.description: True when more rows exist beyond this page

400.description: Bad request — invalid date/operation filter or page

401.description: Unauthorized — missing or invalid API key

get.summary: List the authenticated customer's outbound webhook deliveries across all their registered endpoints

200.description: The caller's own webhook deliveries, newest-first

schema.description: Paginated envelope of the caller's own webhook deliveries

attempts.description: Number of delivery attempts made so far

endpoint_url.description: The registered HTTPS delivery URL the event was sent to

event_id.description: The outbound webhook event type (e.g. invocation.succeeded)

last_response_code.description: HTTP status code returned by the endpoint on the last attempt; absent if no attempt has completed

total.description: Total matching deliveries across all pages

401.description: Unauthorized — missing or invalid API key

get.summary: List the authenticated customer's registered webhook endpoints

200.description: The caller's registered endpoints (secrets never included)

schema.description: Paginated envelope of registered webhook endpoints

items.description: Webhook endpoint objects for this page

total.description: Total registered endpoints across all pages

401.description: Unauthorized — missing or invalid API key

post.summary: Register a new outbound webhook endpoint

subscribed_events.description: Optional subset of event types; omit or null to receive every event type

url.description: Must be https:// and must not resolve to a private/loopback/link-local address

201.description: Endpoint registered; secret_hex is present only in this response

disabled_at.description: RFC3339 timestamp the endpoint was auto-disabled at; null unless currently auto-disabled

disabled_reason.description: Why the endpoint was auto-disabled, e.g. "delivery_failures"; null unless currently auto-disabled

secret_hex.description: Signing secret, hex-encoded. Returned exactly once — store it now, it cannot be retrieved again.

subscribed_events.description: Event types this endpoint receives; null means every event type

400.description: Bad request — invalid url, or unknown subscribed_events entry

401.description: Unauthorized — missing or invalid API key

patch.summary: Replace the subscribed event types for a webhook endpoint owned by the authenticated customer

subscribed_events.description: Replaces the endpoint's subscribed event types; omit or null to resubscribe to every event type

400.description: Bad request — malformed endpoint id, invalid json body, or unknown subscribed_events entry

401.description: Unauthorized — missing or invalid API key

404.description: Endpoint not found (includes ids owned by another customer)

delete.summary: Deactivate a registered webhook endpoint owned by the authenticated customer

401.description: Unauthorized — missing or invalid API key

404.description: Endpoint not found (includes ids owned by another customer)

post.summary: Re-enable a webhook endpoint owned by the authenticated customer that was auto-disabled for chronic delivery failure

401.description: Unauthorized — missing or invalid API key

404.description: Endpoint not found, owned by another customer, or not currently auto-disabled (includes customer soft-deleted endpoints)

post.summary: Rotate the signing secret for a webhook endpoint owned by the authenticated customer

200.description: Secret rotated; secret_hex is present only in this response

secret_hex.description: The new signing secret, hex-encoded. Returned exactly once — store it now, it cannot be retrieved again. The previous secret stops verifying immediately.

401.description: Unauthorized — missing or invalid API key

404.description: Endpoint not found (includes ids owned by another customer)

200.description: Webhook processed

post.summary: Fired when a customer's API key is revoked.
post.description: Delivered only to endpoints subscribed to this event type. An endpoint with no explicit subscribed_events value receives every event type (default).

200.description: The registered endpoint acknowledged the webhook

post.summary: Fired when a customer rotates an API key; the old key enters its grace-period expiry.
post.description: Delivered only to endpoints subscribed to this event type. An endpoint with no explicit subscribed_events value receives every event type (default).

200.description: The registered endpoint acknowledged the webhook

post.summary: Fired when one of the customer's own webhook endpoints auto-disables after exceeding the consecutive delivery-failure threshold. Delivered to the customer's other active endpoints, never to the one just disabled.
post.description: Delivered only to endpoints subscribed to this event type. An endpoint with no explicit subscribed_events value receives every event type (default).

200.description: The registered endpoint acknowledged the webhook

post.summary: Fired when a durable async job reaches the terminal failed state (structured worker error, billable_units<1 contract violation, or retry-exhausted dead-letter).
post.description: Delivered only to endpoints subscribed to this event type. An endpoint with no explicit subscribed_events value receives every event type (default).

error_code.description: The sanitized (or full, per WORKER_ERROR_EXPOSURE) error code

job_id.description: The async job's id; fetch details via GET /v1/jobs/{id}

operation.description: The operation string the job was enqueued with

200.description: The registered endpoint acknowledged the webhook

post.summary: Fired when a durable async job reaches the terminal succeeded state.
post.description: Delivered only to endpoints subscribed to this event type. An endpoint with no explicit subscribed_events value receives every event type (default).

job_id.description: The async job's id; fetch the result via GET /v1/jobs/{id}

operation.description: The operation string the job was enqueued with

200.description: The registered endpoint acknowledged the webhook

post.summary: Fired when a request is rejected because the customer's monthly billable-unit quota is exhausted.
post.description: Delivered only to endpoints subscribed to this event type. An endpoint with no explicit subscribed_events value receives every event type (default).

plan.description: The customer's plan id at the time of rejection

200.description: The registered endpoint acknowledged the webhook

post.summary: Fired when a customer's Stripe subscription is canceled; plan_id reverts to free.
post.description: Delivered only to endpoints subscribed to this event type. An endpoint with no explicit subscribed_events value receives every event type (default).

200.description: The registered endpoint acknowledged the webhook

post.summary: Fired when a customer's Stripe subscription is created or updated (including plan changes).
post.description: Delivered only to endpoints subscribed to this event type. An endpoint with no explicit subscribed_events value receives every event type (default).

plan_id.description: The plan the customer is now on

200.description: The registered endpoint acknowledged the webhook

request_id.description: X-Request-ID value echoed from the request; always present in error responses (value may be empty string if no request ID was generated). Use for support correlation.

ApiKeyAuth.name: X-API-Key
ApiKeyAuth.description: API key for authenticating requests to protected endpoints
